cooked: Sorry to bother everyone with my problems, but LBA text editor (by kakadudu) wouldnt work properly under XP. I used the other one instead and worked fine up until the file reaches a certain size then again it starts to delete characters and put extra ones into the text. I found that the texts are separated by the 001 ASCI code character. One for the text section start and one for the end. Now when the problem occurs the program starts to use a different character for closing the text sections. Now I have the whole file translated, and the division characters are replaced correctly, but the offset values are set wrong and the text editor program can no longer open the file. Neither editors. If anyone could help maybe with this, that would be awsome. Thanx in advance! (as usual)

I found that the texts are separated by the 001 ASCI code character. One for the text section start and one for the end. Now when the problem occurs the program starts to use a different character for closing the text sections. Now I have the whole file translated, and the division characters are replaced correctly, but the offset values are set wrong and the text editor program can no longer open the file. Neither editors. If anyone could help maybe with this, that would be awsome. Thanx in advance! (as usual) :)

cooked:
Originally written here
I recommended you to use LBA Text Editor. If you have Windows XP, LBA Text Editor will not work properly unless you will run it in compatibility mode. To do that: right click on LBATxt.exe (or its shortcut) and click Properties. Click on the Compatibility tab, and choose Windows 95 compatibility mode.

Kakadudus LBA Text should work under XP in this way, and it will probably go much smoother.
raw:
[quote]
[i]Originally written [here](http://www.chaosfish.hpg.ig.com.br/lba/howtocreate.html#text)[/i]
[b]I recommended you to use LBA Text Editor. If you have Windows XP, LBA Text Editor [i]will not work properly[/i] unless you will run it in compatibility mode. To do that: right click on "LBATxt.exe" (or it's shortcut) and click "Properties". Click on the "Compatibility" tab, and choose "Windows 95" compatibility mode.[/b]
[/quote]

Kakadudu's LBA Text should work under XP in this way, and it will probably go much smoother.

cooked: Or maybe you are trying to update FIRST entry in text.hqr. If so then you must know that only even entries are text files, so you must put your first file insted of second entry in text.hqr.
raw: Or maybe you are trying to update FIRST entry in text.hqr. If so then you must know that only even entries are text files, so you must put your first file insted of second entry in text.hqr.

cooked: Sure, but not always every other is text file. I know which ones are text files cuz Text editor can only open text files. And HQR manager shows empty entries. There is no problem with that, but text001.lbt goes to the first entry and contains runtime error messages like You need to insert LBA2 CD into your CD-ROM drive and stuff. But it wouldnt accept it.
raw: Sure, but not always every other is text file. I know which ones are text files cuz Text editor can only open text files. And HQR manager shows empty entries. There is no problem with that, but text001.lbt goes to the first entry and contains runtime error messages like "You need to insert LBA2 CD into your CD-ROM drive" and stuff. But it wouldn't accept it.

cooked:
Err, no. You are wrong. ONLY entries with EVEN indexes contain text (except blank ones ofcourse). Odd entries contain text indexes that are not usable and there is no need to edit them.

The file with error messages is the second one (text002.lbt) and you have to replace the second file with it.

You probably did a mistake with unpacking, thats why this file has text001.lbt name.
raw:
Err, no. You are wrong. ONLY entries with EVEN indexes contain text (except blank ones ofcourse). Odd entries contain text indexes that are not usable and there is no need to edit them.

The file with error messages is the second one (text002.lbt) and you have to replace the second file with it. 

You probably did a mistake with unpacking, that's why this file has "text001.lbt" name.
